notes: Each pattern is a syscall, capability use, exploit-bait input, or file/proc access expected to be blocked by the rootless-container + seccomp + dropped-caps + cgroup defense per threat-model §3.5. Expected runtime defense: (a) the syscall ENOSYS or EPERM from the seccomp profile; (b) capability EPERM from dropped-caps; (c) cgroup OOM/PIDS limit kicks; (d) falco emits a Critical event and the container is killed by the runner supervisor within 2s. Verify's spec mocks the runner with synthetic stimuli (e.g., a binary calling unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER)) and asserts (i) the syscall fails with the expected errno, (ii) a falco event is emitted to audit_logs, (iii) the run terminates in 'killed' state, (iv) no host-side artifacts (process, file outside the per-run tmpfs) survive. M2 exit-gate: top-30 patterns 100% blocked (per sprint/milestone-M2.md line 64). M5 exit-gate: full ≥200 corpus + external pentest sign-off.

patterns.pattern: syscall: mount("none", "/mnt", "tmpfs", 0, NULL) — requires CAP_SYS_ADMIN
patterns.expected_outcome: Dropped cap CAP_SYS_ADMIN → mount() returns EPERM (errno 1). Seccomp also denies mount() explicitly. Falco rule 'Mount Launched in Privileged Container' fires Critical.
patterns.notes: Threat-model SE-4 (capability-as-escape-primitive). CAP_SYS_ADMIN is the most-abused capability in container escapes (CIS Docker Benchmark §5.22). Mount is the canonical exercise.

patterns.pattern: syscall: socket(AF_PACKET, SOCK_RAW, 0) — requires CAP_NET_RAW
patterns.expected_outcome: CAP_NET_RAW dropped → socket(AF_PACKET) returns EPERM. Raw packet capture / ARP-spoof primitives blocked.
patterns.notes: Threat-model §3.5 mitigation #2. CAP_NET_RAW is the cap that enables packet spoofing and L2 attacks; runner has no network-debugging need so dropped.

patterns.pattern: syscall: open("/etc/shadow", O_RDONLY) — requires CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH if file not world-readable
patterns.expected_outcome: CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH dropped → open returns EACCES on root-owned files outside the runner's mount namespace. Also: read-only-rootfs prevents writes regardless.
patterns.notes: DAC override primitives must be off — without them, even host-process compromises can't read root-owned files.

patterns.pattern: syscall: setuid(0) — requires CAP_SETUID
patterns.expected_outcome: CAP_SETUID dropped → setuid returns EPERM. Even if the runner process is somehow elevated, it cannot become real root.
patterns.notes: Rootless container UID 0 maps to unprivileged host UID, but in-container CAP_SETUID would still allow internal user-switching primitives — dropped.

patterns.pattern: syscall: ptrace(PTRACE_ATTACH, target_pid, NULL, NULL) — requires CAP_SYS_PTRACE
patterns.expected_outcome: CAP_SYS_PTRACE dropped → ptrace returns EPERM. /proc/sys/kernel/yama/ptrace_scope=2 belt-and-suspenders.
patterns.notes: CVE-2022-0492 was a cgroups-v1 escape that used ptrace primitives in concert; both vectors closed by dropping CAP_SYS_PTRACE.

patterns.pattern: syscall: init_module(...) — requires CAP_SYS_MODULE
patterns.expected_outcome: CAP_SYS_MODULE dropped → init_module returns EPERM. Container cannot load kernel modules even if attacker controls kernel-object bytes.
patterns.notes: Kernel-module loading is a direct host RCE if reachable. Always dropped.

patterns.pattern: syscall: unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER | CLONE_NEWNS) — attempt new-namespace creation
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp profile explicitly denies 'unshare' → ENOSYS (errno 38). Falco rule 'Namespace Creation Inside Container' fires.
patterns.notes: Threat-model SE-2 (the named seccomp deny). CVE-2022-0492 (user-namespace cgroup escape) used unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER) as the privilege-escalation primitive; closed by seccomp deny.

patterns.pattern: syscall: setns(fd, CLONE_NEWNS) — join host namespace
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies 'setns' → ENOSYS. Even with a leaked host-namespace fd, the syscall fails.
patterns.notes: setns is the 'enter another namespace' primitive — fundamental escape building block. Threat-model §3.5 #3.

patterns.pattern: syscall: bpf(BPF_PROG_LOAD, &attr, sizeof(attr)) — load eBPF program
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies 'bpf' → ENOSYS. Defends against eBPF-verifier-bypass class CVEs (e.g., CVE-2021-3490 ALU32 bounds-tracking).
patterns.notes: eBPF has been a steady source of LPE CVEs (CVE-2021-3490, CVE-2021-31440, CVE-2022-23222). Disabling bpf() in seccomp eliminates the entire surface.

patterns.pattern: syscall: keyctl(KEYCTL_ADD_KEY, ...) — kernel keyring manipulation
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies 'keyctl' → ENOSYS. Defends against the kernel-keyring race-condition CVE class.
patterns.notes: CVE-2016-0728, CVE-2022-1116 — kernel keyring has historically been an LPE target. Disabling keyctl() narrows attack surface.

patterns.pattern: syscall: pivot_root("/newroot", "/oldroot") — chroot-style root pivot
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies 'pivot_root' → ENOSYS. Plus CAP_SYS_ADMIN is dropped (required for pivot_root) — double-lock.
patterns.notes: pivot_root is the OCI runtime's own primitive for setting up the container — closed for in-container code so it can't be re-pointed.

patterns.pattern: syscall: kexec_load(...) — load new kernel image
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies 'kexec_load' → ENOSYS.
patterns.notes: kexec is the 'soft reboot into a new kernel' primitive. Catastrophic if reachable; always blocked.

patterns.pattern: Write to /sys/fs/cgroup/release_agent (cgroup-v1 notify_on_release abuse)
patterns.expected_outcome: Read-only rootfs and read-only /sys mount → write returns EROFS. cgroup-v2-only configuration also closes the surface entirely.
patterns.notes: CVE-2022-0492 — cgroup-v1 release_agent abuse for container escape. Tracee/falco rule 'cgroup notify_on_release' detects.

patterns.pattern: Mount cgroup-v1 filesystem with rw inside container: mount -t cgroup -o rw none /tmp/cg
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies mount + cap drop blocks. Even if attempted via direct syscall: EPERM.
patterns.notes: Pre-condition for cgroup-release_agent abuse — the mount itself is the first lock.

patterns.pattern: Fork bomb to exhaust container PID-cgroup: while(1) fork()
patterns.expected_outcome: cgroup pids.max = 1000 → fork returns EAGAIN. Container is OOM-killed by the runner supervisor at PID-saturation threshold; host unaffected.
patterns.notes: Threat-model SE-5 (DoS class). cgroup pids controller is the dedicated defense.

patterns.pattern: Memory exhaustion: malloc(1 << 40) loop attempting host OOM
patterns.expected_outcome: cgroup memory.max = 4GB → allocations beyond cap return ENOMEM. OOM-killer scoped to container only. Host memory pressure unchanged.
patterns.notes: Threat-model SE-5 (the memory variant). Containerized OOM never reaches host.

patterns.pattern: Dirty Pipe (CVE-2022-0847): splice() a writable pipe over a read-only page-cache page to overwrite root-owned file
patterns.expected_outcome: Host kernel patched (post-5.16.11 / 5.15.25 / 5.10.102). Verify spec asserts kernel >= patched minimum at runner startup; runner refuses to launch on unpatched kernel. Additionally, read-only root filesystem prevents the writeback target from existing inside the container's writable layer.
patterns.notes: CVE-2022-0847 Dirty Pipe — Max Kellermann's discovery; widely-exploited container escape vector through 2022-2023. Mitigation is kernel patch + read-only rootfs.

patterns.pattern: nf_tables OOB write (CVE-2024-1086): trigger universe-after-free in nftables via crafted netlink message
patterns.expected_outcome: Two locks: (a) seccomp denies socket(AF_NETLINK, ...) for non-runner-internal use; (b) host kernel patched (post-6.4.16 / 5.15.149 backport). Runner-startup kernel-version check refuses to run on unpatched.
patterns.notes: CVE-2024-1086 — LPE-to-root via nftables. Heavily exploited 2024-2025. Egress-allowlist also makes the netlink AF socket unnecessary.

patterns.pattern: io_uring abuse (CVE-2023-2598): io_uring fixed-buffer registration race for kernel memory R/W
patterns.expected_outcome: Seccomp denies io_uring_setup, io_uring_register, io_uring_enter → ENOSYS. io_uring entirely off in the runner (we don't need its perf benefits for read-only ingest).
patterns.notes: CVE-2023-2598 + CVE-2024-0582 — io_uring is a recurring CVE source; disabling at seccomp level is the durable fix.

patterns.pattern: Classic chroot escape via fchdir(open(".")) after creating a sub-chroot and chdir-ing out
patterns.expected_outcome: rootless container is not a chroot — it's a mount-namespace + user-namespace combo. Classic chroot tricks fail by design. Plus: CAP_SYS_CHROOT dropped → chroot() inside container returns EPERM.
patterns.notes: The 'open the parent then escape' trick (Goldberg & Wagner 1996 'A Secure Environment for Untrusted Helper Applications') doesn't apply to namespaces — included as a sanity check that ops haven't downgraded to chroot.

patterns.pattern: mknod-then-read /dev/sda1 inside container
patterns.expected_outcome: Devices cgroup denies mknod for block devices; CAP_MKNOD dropped. Even if a device node were created, read returns EACCES (we never grant access to host disks).
patterns.notes: Direct host-disk read is a chroot-escape sibling — namespace-isolation alone doesn't stop it without device cgroup + cap drop.

patterns.pattern: Bind-mount host /etc via mount("/", "/tmp/host", NULL, MS_BIND, NULL)
patterns.expected_outcome: CAP_SYS_ADMIN dropped + seccomp denies mount → EPERM. Host filesystem invisible regardless.
patterns.notes: Bind-mount-the-host is the 'I have CAP_SYS_ADMIN' escape primitive — closed by cap drop.

patterns.pattern: /proc/self/exe overwrite (CVE-2019-5736 runc-style): overwrite the runtime's host binary by writing through /proc/self/exe of the runc init process
patterns.expected_outcome: Rootless container — there is no real root, /proc/self/exe of the host runtime maps to an unprivileged host UID's binary. Write to /proc/self/exe also returns ETXTBSY on a running binary. Plus: runc and Podman are post-1.0.0-rc6 / >=2.0 (patched).
patterns.notes: CVE-2019-5736 (Adam Iwaniuk + Borys Popławski) — the canonical container-runtime CVE. Rootless is the architectural fix; runtime version is the patch lock.

patterns.pattern: Symlink race in runc volume mount (CVE-2024-21626): /proc/self/fd/N file descriptor leak to attach to host filesystem
patterns.expected_outcome: runc >= 1.1.12 (patched); runner startup asserts version. Read-only rootfs + dropped caps form belt-and-suspenders.
patterns.notes: CVE-2024-21626 'Leaky Vessels' — Snyk Jan 2024. Multi-runtime CVE (runc, BuildKit, Moby). Patching is the only fix; the runner enforces version at boot.

patterns.pattern: BuildKit GRPC mount-API abuse (CVE-2024-23651): race between SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH check and mount
patterns.expected_outcome: We do not run BuildKit in the runner — runner is execute-not-build (D9: no npm install). BuildKit binary not present in runner image.
patterns.notes: CVE-2024-23651 'Leaky Vessels' part 2. Mitigation is architectural: runner doesn't build images.

patterns.pattern: Docker socket exposed at /var/run/docker.sock — attempt to spawn a privileged sibling container
patterns.expected_outcome: Docker socket NEVER mounted into the runner container. CI grep test asserts no '/var/run/docker.sock' volume in the runner Helm chart / docker-compose.
patterns.notes: Docker-socket-mount is the 'instant root' antipattern. Forbidden by config; CI gate enforces.

patterns.pattern: Heap spray triggering libxml2 use-after-free (CVE-2023-39615) via crafted XML in a customer file
patterns.expected_outcome: (a) Runner uses tree-sitter for parsing, not libxml2 — surface eliminated. (b) Even if a transitive dep pulls libxml2, version-pinning + Renovate auto-bump keeps post-patch (>=2.11.5). (c) RCE attempt within container hits seccomp + dropped-caps; falco detects /bin/sh exec and kills container.
patterns.notes: CVE-2023-39615 — libxml2 UAF in xmlSchemaIDCFillNodeTables. Composability point: parser CVE in a transitive dep is mitigated by container-level defense even if the parser is exploited.

patterns.pattern: Stack overflow in OpenSSL ASN.1 parsing (CVE-2023-0286) via X.509 verification on a customer-supplied .pem
patterns.expected_outcome: Runner does not invoke X.509 verification on customer-supplied keys — keys are surfaced as findings, not parsed. OpenSSL >= 3.0.8 (patched) in runner image regardless. Container defense kicks in for hypothetical RCE.
patterns.notes: CVE-2023-0286 — OpenSSL X.400 GeneralName type-confusion. Architectural fix: don't parse customer cryptographic material.

patterns.pattern: Crafted GIF triggering libtiff buffer overflow (CVE-2023-0800) processed by an unintended dep chain
patterns.expected_outcome: Runner ingests text only — binary asset files (images, PDFs, archives) are listed in findings but not parsed. libtiff is not in the runner image's dependency closure (asserted by CI: 'ldd runner | grep libtiff' empty).
patterns.notes: CVE-2023-0800 — libtiff is a CVE-rich dep; architectural fix is to not pull it in.

patterns.pattern: Read /proc/kallsyms to leak kernel addresses for ROP gadget construction
patterns.expected_outcome: /proc/kallsyms returns all-zero addresses to unprivileged readers (kernel.kptr_restrict=2). Container is unprivileged → all zeroes returned. KASLR active.
patterns.notes: Pre-exploit info-leak: even if kernel exploit is available, address leak is needed. kptr_restrict closes it.

patterns.pattern: Write to /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern to redirect coredumps to attacker-controlled handler
patterns.expected_outcome: /proc/sys is read-only mounted; write returns EROFS. CAP_SYS_ADMIN dropped (required for sysctl writes). cgroup-v2 unified hierarchy denies this even when readable.
patterns.notes: CVE-2022-0492 used core_pattern + release_agent in concert — both closed.

patterns.pattern: Read /proc/<host-pid>/root/etc/shadow via leaked host PID namespace reference
patterns.expected_outcome: PID namespace is isolated — /proc only shows in-container PIDs. There is no host-PID-namespace reference in scope. Plus: file mode 0640 owned by root would fail open even if reached (CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH dropped).
patterns.notes: Cross-PID-namespace /proc read is a classic shared-PID-ns escape; PID namespace isolation closes it.

patterns.pattern: Headless Chromium renderer-sandbox-escape via WebGL CVE-2024-0519 (V8 OOB read) on a Surface-audit page
patterns.expected_outcome: Chromium pinned to post-122.0.6261.94 (patched). Chromium runs with --renderer-sandbox enabled (CI grep test enforces '--no-sandbox' is forbidden — threat-model §3.5 mitigation #9). Browser process is in a separate cgroup with the same seccomp+caps profile. RCE-via-render is contained.
patterns.notes: Threat-model SE-6. CVE-2024-0519 V8 OOB read — one of the heavily exploited Chrome 0-days. Two locks: pin + sandbox flag.
